## C → Rust Conversion Batch

This PR was automatically generated by the `linux-c2rust-pipeline`.

### What this PR contains
- Transpiled Rust files (unsafe, generated by `c2rust`)
- Post-processed idiom transforms applied
- Baseline unsafe block counts logged

### Required before merge
- [ ] Stage 2 (Security) gate must pass
- [ ] Stage 3 (Compatibility) gate must pass
- [ ] Stage 4 (Build & Boot) gate must pass
- [ ] Human review of unsafe block justifications
- [ ] `// SAFETY:` comments reviewed by a kernel maintainer

### Known limitations of auto-transpilation
- Generated code uses `unsafe` extensively — this is expected
- `c2rust` output is **not** idiomatic Rust and must be reviewed
- FFI types may need manual adjustment for kernel-specific ABI
- Stack allocation patterns may need conversion to kernel heap APIs

### Reviewer checklist
- [ ] All `unsafe` blocks have `// SAFETY:` rationale
- [ ] No `std::` imports (kernel has no std)
- [ ] No `panic!`/`unwrap()` (use `Result` / kernel error types)
- [ ] Memory allocation uses `kernel::alloc` not `std::alloc`
- [ ] Lock types use `kernel::sync` primitives
- [ ] Integer arithmetic is overflow-checked
